title: [security] EDHOC signature verification incompatible with Schnorr48
description: Commit 789c92645 updated lichen/subsys/lichen/edhoc/include/lichen/edhoc.h:48 to define EDHOC_SIG_LEN=48 and edhoc.c:752 to check signature_2.len != EDHOC_SIG_LEN. However, the local ed25519_verify() at edhoc.c:492 (sig param typed [64]), 792, and 1365 still calls crypto_ed25519_check() expecting full 64-byte Ed25519 signature. Schnorr48 (per draft-lichen-schnorr-00.md: 16B challenge + 32B response, test/vectors/schnorr48.json) is incompatible. This can lead to incorrect verification (forgery possible), buffer overread, or timing sidechannels. The SECURITY: constant-time comment is now inaccurate for this path. Must replace with schnorr48_verify() from lichen/subsys/lichen/link/schnorr48.h (which has dedicated test in lichen/tests/schnorr48/). Also affects key handling in EDHOC context and MIC-like MAC_2/3 computations. No similar issue in Rust lichen-oscore/edhoc.rs which uses proper SIG_LEN. Related to Schnorr, EDHOC, crypto timing, key handling. See lichen_node_init() ordering too.
